text.title: Brainstorming Techniques Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding brainstorming-techniques. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The session plan MUST forbid evaluative language during generation phase. Phrases 'yes, but', 'that won't work' trigger facilitator intervention.
rule.rationale: Judgment kills wild ideas; quantity correlates with quality only when evaluation is deferred.

rule.statement: Session plan MUST set a numeric quantity target (e.g. ≥50 ideas in 30 min for Classic, 108 for 6-3-5). 'Brainstorm until done' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a target, group stops at the first 'good enough' batch and misses the long tail where novel ideas live.

rule.statement: Technique MUST match group profile: Classic for 4-8 extroverts, 6-3-5 for written-comfortable teams, Round Robin for mixed-seniority where dominance is a risk, Reverse for risk-surfacing.
rule.rationale: Wrong technique under-uses the group; Round Robin in 10-extroverts is glacial, Classic in 6-introverts is silent.

rule.statement: Generation prompt MUST include 'Yes, and...' build cue. Idea cards MUST permit linking to parent idea for traceability of builds.
rule.rationale: Builds drive 30-40% of total idea count and produce the highest-rated combinations.

rule.statement: Post-generation, ideas MUST be deduplicated, clustered into 3-7 themes, and scored on impact × effort. Raw lists are forbidden as session output.
rule.rationale: Unclustered lists are not actionable; clustering surfaces themes and forces explicit prioritization.
